mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding output capacity and aggregate supply, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO is above its 3-month moving average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market weakness that erodes consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After trigger activation, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) for two consecutive months indicate a broad demand contraction that precedes equity market weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: When the joint trigger fires, S&P 500 delivers positive forward 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or the cumulative return in triggered windows is not statistically lower than non-triggered windows (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds the year-over-year CPI inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) by at least 200 basis points, monetary policy is restrictive and compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition holds, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative, or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 45% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects robust aggregate demand that underpins corporate earnings expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDPC1 annualized growth rate and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.2) or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment (PAYEMS) increase exceeding 200,000 jobs indicates a resilient labor market that supports consumer income and spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After trigger, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) are above their respective 6-month moving averages, the economy is in a synchronous expansion phase that lifts corporate profits and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and RSAFS above 6-month moving average
failure_condition: When both proxies are above their MAs, the S&P 500 yields negative average 20-day returns, or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 4.0% and CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) month-over-month exceeds 0.3%, the economy is overheating with tight labor markets and rising price pressures that force policy tightening and erode equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle overheating with tight labor market
failure_condition: In periods meeting the trigger condition, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive more than 60% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
